perturbation.original_text: 16. Governing Law. The terms of this Agreement shall be construed in accordance with the laws of Singapore . All disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall be finally settled under the Rules of Arbitration of the International Chamber of Commerce by 3 (three) arbitrators appointed in accordance with the said Rules. The seat, or legal pla ce, of arbitration shall be Singapore , Singapore . The language to be used in the arbitral proceedings shall be English. The foregoing, however, shall not preclude the Parties from applying for a preliminary or injunctive remedy available under applicable l aws for any purpose.
perturbation.changed_text:
1. Definitions. For purposes of this Agreement the following terms have the meanings specified for them: 1.1. “Affiliate ” means any corporation, company or other entity that directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with a party to this Agreement. An entity is regarded as in control of another or owning another if it owns or controls, directly or indirectly, fifty percent (50%) or more of the shares entitled to vo te for the election of directors or other persons performing similar functions. 1.2. “ Confidential Information ” – Confidential Information is any information of the Discloser of actual or potential commercial value owing to the fact that it is unknown to t hird parties; disclosed by the Discloser to the Recipient in writing, by way of document delivery, including in an electronic format safeguarded from unauthorised access to the information in transit; related to business or financial plans or strategies, f inancial reports or accounting (except to the extent contemplated by the law), contractual relations, pricing and marketing of goods, products or services, technical information, commercial secrets, know -how, research, production plans, concepts, intellect ual property items (including inventions, discoveries, innovative ideas, useful models, designs, production prototypes not covered by patent for whatever reason, computer programmes, databases, trademark designs not registered for whatever reason).
16. Governing Law. The terms of this Agreement shall be construed in accordance with the laws of Singapore . All disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall be finally settled under the Rules of Arbitration of the International Chamber of Commerce by 3 (three) arbitrators appointed in accordance with the said Rules. The seat, or legal pla ce, of arbitration shall be Singapore , Singapore . The language to be used in the arbitral proceedings shall be English. The foregoing, however, shall not preclude the Parties from applying for a preliminary or injunctive remedy available under applicable l aws for any purpose.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the governing law and arbitration clause to the 'Definitions' section makes it harder to find. Parties may not realize that Singapore law governs the contract or that disputes must be arbitrated. This can lead to confusion and potential legal challenges.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 6 of the Arbitration Act deals with the enforceability of arbitration agreements. Placing the governing law and arbitration clauses within definitions, instead of a clearly labeled section on dispute resolution, undermines the intention of the Act by making the agreement less conspicuous and potentially less enforceable.

"Maritime transactions" and "commerce" defined; exceptions to operation of title "Maritime transactions", as herein defined, means charter parties, bills of lading of water carriers, agreements relating to wharfage, supplies furnished vessels or repairs to vessels, collisions, or any other matters in foreign commerce which, if the subject of controversy, would be embraced within admiralty jurisdiction; "commerce", as herein defined, means commerce among the several States or with foreign nations, or in any Territory of the United States or in the District of Columbia, or between any such Territory and another, or between any such Territory and any State or foreign nation, or between the District of Columbia and any State or Territory or foreign nation, but nothing herein contained shall apply to contracts of employment of seamen, railroad employees, or any other class of workers engaged in foreign or interstate commerce. perturbation.justification: The perturbation moves the governing law and arbitration clause into the definitions section, making them less conspicuous. This undermines the intent of ensuring parties are aware of their rights and obligations, which can be tied to the enforceability of agreements. While the scraped snippets do not explicitly state where these clauses must be located, the Law Explanation suggests this placement undermines the enforceability of the arbitration agreement, implicitly suggesting a violation of the principles of fair notice and transparency that underpin contract law. Placing crucial legal stipulations within definitions deviates from standard practices and could be construed as an attempt to obscure these terms. This makes the arbitration agreement potentially less enforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 13. Duration of Obligations. Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement end five (5) years after the date of first disclosure hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text:
18. Severability. If a court of competent jurisdiction determines that any provision of this Agreement is unenforceable, invalid or illegal, it will be severed with the rest of this Agreement remaining in full force and effect.
13. Duration of Obligations. Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement end five (5) years after the date of first disclosure hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the duration of obligations clause to the 'Severability' section reduces its visibility. It is vital for both parties to know how long the NDA lasts, and hiding it in a section about severability creates confusion.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 6 of the Limitation Act defines limitation periods for contract actions. Moving the duration of obligations clause to 'Severability' makes it less obvious when the NDA expires, which could impact the statute of limitations for bringing a claim of breach of the agreement. This goes against the purpose of the Limitation Act, which seeks to provide clarity on the timeframes for legal action. The duration of the contract helps determine the limitations period.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: statute of limitations | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you statute of limitations A statute of limitations is any law that bars claims after a certain period of time passes after an injury. The period of time varies depending on the jurisdiction and the type of claim. There are statutes of limitations for both civil and criminal causes of action . They may begin to run from the date of the injury, the date it was discovered, or the date on which it would have been discovered with reasonable efforts. Many statutes of limitations are actual legislative statutes , while others may come from judicial common law . Compare: s tatute of repose . [Last reviewed in June of 2024 by the Wex Definitions Team ] Wex THE LEGAL PROCESS courts statutory interpretation wex definitions courts and procedure criminal law and procedure legal education and practice statutes Wex Toolbox
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a structural flaw by moving the 'Duration of Obligations' clause into the 'Severability' section, diminishing its visibility. This undermines the principle of clarity regarding the limitation period for bringing a claim, potentially conflicting with the purpose of statutes of limitations as described in Scraped Law Snippet 2: 'A statute of limitations is any law that bars claims after a certain period of time passes after an injury.' By obscuring the contract's duration, the changed text makes it more difficult to determine when the statute of limitations begins, creating a legal contradiction as the clause is now harder to locate and interpret.
